title: quercus aquatica var. heterophylla
intro: Quercus aquatica var. heterophylla, also known as the water oak or swamp oak, is a deciduous tree species native to the southeastern United States. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include astringent, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects, making it useful in treating wounds, infections, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Quercus aquatica var. heterophylla include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support the immune system. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often combined with other herbs in traditional medicine to enhance its effects.
